title: auth-service ConfigMap key removed after deploy
symptoms: Pods restarted after deploy; logs showed KeyError OIDC_ISSUER_URL; auth callback 500s increased.
root_cause: ConfigMap cleanup removed OIDC_ISSUER_URL from production auth-service manifest.
verification_commands: kubectl -n production describe pod -l app=auth-service
kubectl -n production get configmap auth-service-config -o yaml
kubectl rollout history deploy/auth-service -n production
resolution: Restored ConfigMap key from previous release and restarted only affected pods.
lessons_learned: Add config schema validation in CI and alert annotations for missing env vars.
dead_ends: forced pod restart without restoring the ConfigMap key, pods crash-looped again immediately

title: checkout-service CrashLoopBackOff due to bad environment variable
symptoms: Checkout pods restarted every minute; 5xx spike started directly after deploy; logs showed invalid PAYMENT_PROVIDER_URL.
root_cause: New deployment set PAYMENT_PROVIDER_URL to an empty string.
verification_commands: kubectl -n production logs deploy/checkout-service --previous --tail=200
kubectl -n production describe deploy/checkout-service
kubectl -n production get events --sort-by=.lastTimestamp | grep checkout-service
resolution: Rolled back deployment, restored env var, and verified checkout synthetic transaction.
lessons_learned: Deploy gates should validate required environment variables before rollout.
dead_ends: restarted only the affected pods, did not fix it because the env var was empty across the deployment
rolled back to previous image but config drift persisted, root cause was the new ConfigMap

title: payments-api database connection pool exhausted
symptoms: Payments timed out with 503; logs showed timeout waiting for connection from pool; database active sessions near max.
root_cause: Worker release increased pool size per pod while autoscaler doubled replicas.
verification_commands: kubectl -n production logs deploy/payments-api | grep -i 'pool\|connection'
psql -c "select state,count(*) from pg_stat_activity group by 1;"
kubectl -n production get hpa payments-api
resolution: Reduced app pool size, capped HPA temporarily, and terminated idle sessions after approval.
lessons_learned: Pool budget must be global across replicas, not per pod.
dead_ends: increased per-pod pool size, made it worse because replicas multiplied
scaled DB vertically, did not help since contention was on the pool not the DB

title: worker-service Redis timeout after node restart
symptoms: Background job latency increased after node restart; Redis endpoint had stale DNS entries.
root_cause: Worker DNS cache held old Redis node IP after managed node restart.
verification_commands: kubectl -n production logs deploy/worker-service | grep -i redis
kubectl -n production exec deploy/worker-service -- nslookup redis
redis-cli --latency
resolution: Restarted workers gradually and reduced DNS cache TTL.
lessons_learned: Runbook now checks DNS cache after dependency node replacement.
dead_ends: restarted Redis pod, did not help since the cache was on the worker DNS resolver
increased Redis client timeout, only delayed the symptom

title: frontend 5xx spike from feature flag
symptoms: 5xx increased after feature flag checkout_redesign enabled; JavaScript exception referenced undefined cart.total.
root_cause: Feature flag exposed component path that assumed optional cart field was always present.
verification_commands: Check feature flag audit log for checkout_redesign
Query frontend error events for cart.total
Compare 5xx by flag cohort
resolution: Disabled flag and patched null handling before gradual re-enable.
lessons_learned: Add cohort-specific alerting and typed contract tests for flagged components.
dead_ends: rolled back the entire deploy, but the issue was a flag toggle not a code change

title: orders-api migration lock timeout
symptoms: Deploy stalled; orders writes returned 500; database migration waited on lock.
root_cause: Online migration attempted ALTER TABLE while long-running report query held lock.
verification_commands: psql -c "select * from pg_locks where not granted;"
kubectl -n production logs job/orders-migration
Check deploy timeline for migration start
resolution: Cancelled report query, reran migration with lock timeout and off-peak schedule.
lessons_learned: Require lock-safe migration templates and preflight lock checks.
dead_ends: killed the migration job, left the schema in a half-applied state

title: notifications-service expired SMTP credential
symptoms: Email delivery failed; logs showed SMTP auth rejected; credential expired overnight.
root_cause: SMTP credential rotation reminder was not wired to alerting.
verification_commands: kubectl -n production logs deploy/notifications-service | grep -i smtp
Check secret metadata for expiration
Send test email through staging account
resolution: Rotated SMTP secret and restarted notifications-service.
lessons_learned: Track credential expiry as SLO-backed operational event.

title: identity-service suspicious login spike
symptoms: Login failures spiked from many IPs; successful logins from unusual geographies appeared in small number.
root_cause: Credential stuffing campaign using reused passwords.
verification_commands: Query auth logs by IP, ASN, user-agent, and account
Export successful login event IDs for review
Check MFA challenge and reset events
resolution: Applied targeted rate limits, forced password reset for impacted accounts, and preserved evidence bundle.
lessons_learned: Add adaptive rate limits by ASN and impossible-travel alert correlation.
dead_ends: broad IP ban, hit legitimate users from same ASN
force password reset before MFA enrollment, locked out non-affected accounts

title: waf SQL injection alert requiring enrichment
symptoms: WAF grouped repeated SQLi payloads against /search; app 500s appeared for subset of request IDs.
root_cause: Malicious probes hit search endpoint; app handled blocked requests poorly and logged stack traces.
verification_commands: Export WAF event IDs and raw request samples
Join request IDs against api-gateway and app logs
Check database logs for matching queries
resolution: Confirmed WAF blocks, added targeted block rule, patched search error handling.
lessons_learned: Security alerts should enrich request IDs before recommending containment.
dead_ends: disabled WAF rule, removed protection from a real attack
blocked source ASN entirely, took out a CDN edge serving real traffic

title: api-gateway JWT validation failures after key rotation
symptoms: 401s increased after key rotation; old JWKS cached by gateway pods; auth boundary changed.
root_cause: Gateway JWKS cache TTL exceeded key overlap window.
verification_commands: kubectl -n production logs deploy/api-gateway | grep -i jwks
Compare identity-service key rotation timestamp with gateway cache TTL
curl /.well-known/jwks.json from gateway pod
resolution: Flushed gateway cache, extended key overlap, and verified token validation for old/new keys.
lessons_learned: Key rotation runbook must include cache TTL and overlap checks.
dead_ends: bumped JWKS cache TTL aggressively, broke other gateway pods that needed the old keys
force-restarted all gateway pods, re-cached the same stale JWKS

title: catalog-api OOMKilled after search index rollout
symptoms: Pods OOMKilled after new search index loaded; memory doubled during warmup.
root_cause: Search index warmup loaded both old and new index concurrently beyond pod memory limit.
verification_commands: kubectl -n production describe pod -l app=catalog-api | grep -i oom -C 3
kubectl -n production top pod -l app=catalog-api
Check release notes for index warmup
resolution: Rolled back index loader and raised memory after profiling.
lessons_learned: Canary memory-heavy startup paths before global rollout.
dead_ends: increased memory limit blindly, OOM still recurred during warmup

title: billing-worker bad liveness probe caused restart loop
symptoms: Worker restarted while processing long jobs; liveness endpoint blocked on job queue.
root_cause: Liveness probe depended on Redis and long-running job health instead of process health.
verification_commands: kubectl -n production describe pod -l app=billing-worker
kubectl -n production get events --sort-by=.lastTimestamp | grep Liveness
Review probe path and timeoutSeconds
resolution: Changed liveness to process-only endpoint and moved dependency checks to readiness.
lessons_learned: Liveness probes must not depend on downstream services.
dead_ends: increased liveness timeoutSeconds, only stretched the restart loop

title: search-service dependency timeout to vector database
symptoms: Search p95 latency exceeded 6s; vector database timeout errors increased.
root_cause: Vector DB index compaction saturated IO and client timeout was too low for degraded mode.
verification_commands: kubectl -n production logs deploy/search-service | grep -i timeout
Check vector DB compaction metrics
Compare timeout errors by endpoint
resolution: Paused compaction, increased client timeout temporarily, and enabled degraded keyword-only fallback.
lessons_learned: Runbooks should include degraded search mode switch.
dead_ends: scaled search-service replicas, only multiplied vector DB load

title: inventory-service config mistake changed Kafka topic
symptoms: Inventory updates stopped; consumer lag grew; deployment pointed consumer to staging Kafka topic.
root_cause: Environment overlay selected wrong Kafka topic name.
verification_commands: kubectl -n production get deploy inventory-service -o yaml | grep KAFKA_TOPIC
kafka-consumer-groups --describe --group inventory-service
Check kustomize/helm values diff
resolution: Corrected topic config and replayed missed messages from retained offset.
lessons_learned: Add environment allowlist validation for topic names.

title: realtime-api failed deploy due to image pull secret
symptoms: New pods stuck ImagePullBackOff; existing pods served traffic but rollout stalled.
root_cause: Registry token expired and image pull secret was not rotated.
verification_commands: kubectl -n production describe pod -l app=realtime-api
kubectl -n production get secret registry-pull -o yaml
Check registry token expiration
resolution: Rotated image pull secret and resumed rollout.

title: admin-api elevated 403s after RBAC policy change
symptoms: Admin users received 403 after RBAC policy deploy; security-sensitive access control changed.
root_cause: Policy bundle omitted support-admin role mapping.
verification_commands: Check policy bundle diff
Query 403 logs by role and endpoint
Verify no privilege escalation paths were opened
resolution: Rolled back policy bundle and added regression test for support-admin role.
lessons_learned: RBAC deploys require both deny and allow regression tests.
dead_ends: overrode the policy bundle, but the regression was a missing role mapping not a global block

title: webhook-service third-party timeout after provider incident
symptoms: Webhook retries piled up; provider status page reported degraded API; queue age increased.
root_cause: Third-party provider latency caused retry storm due to aggressive retry policy.
verification_commands: kubectl -n production logs deploy/webhook-service | grep -i timeout
Check provider status page and response latency
Inspect queue age and retry counts
resolution: Raised retry backoff, paused non-critical webhooks, and replayed queue after recovery.
lessons_learned: Retry policy should include circuit breaker and provider-specific status checks.
dead_ends: hit the queue with manual replays, multiplied the retry storm

title: profile-service cache stampede after deploy
symptoms: Database CPU spiked after deploy; cache hit rate dropped; profile reads slowed.
root_cause: Deploy changed cache key prefix, invalidating hot cache for all users simultaneously.
verification_commands: Check cache hit rate around deploy
Compare cache key prefix diff
Query DB CPU and top statements
resolution: Rolled back prefix, warmed cache for hot keys, and throttled profile reads.
lessons_learned: Cache key changes need gradual migration and warmup plan.
dead_ends: scaled the database vertically, did not address the root cause which was cache key invalidation
